definition: A condition in which the blood doesn't have enough healthy red blood cells.
basic: Not enough red blood in body.
pro: The patient is showing signs of anemia due to iron deficiency.
insight: Commonly used in clinical reports and nursing notes.

definition: A condition in which the force of the blood against the artery walls is too high.
pro: Chronic hypertension can lead to severe cardiovascular complications.
insight: Use this instead of 'high blood pressure' in formal medical charts.

definition: The process of determining the priority of patients' treatments based on the severity of their condition.
pro: The emergency department established a new triage protocol.
insight: Essential vocabulary for nurses working in ER or high-volume clinics.

pro: The patient remained asymptomatic throughout the observation period.
insight: A critical term for describing patient status without physical symptoms.

definition: A condition that comes on suddenly and is often severe.
pro: The patient was admitted with acute abdominal pain.
